page.text: Let $X$ be a set, $a \in X$, and $f \colon X \to X$. Then there exists a function $u \colon \omega \to X$ such that $u(0) = a$ and $u(n^+) = f(u(n))$ for all $n \in \omega$. The function $u$ is built as the intersection of all subsets of $\omega \times X$ that contain $(0, a)$ and are closed under the rule sending $(n, x)$ to $(n^+, f(x))$; induction shows this intersection is a function. Recursion is the tool used to define arithmetic on the natural numbers.
